type_system_changes.union_types: Use union types when docblocks express type unions

namespace_updates.fully_qualified: Use explicit use statements for external classes

error_handling.warnings_to_exceptions: Audit warnings promoted to TypeError/ValueError in 8.x

syntax_changes.array_string_access: Prefer explicit braces and avoid ambiguous offset access
syntax_changes.named_arguments: Named arguments are supported in PHP 8.0+
syntax_changes.match_expression: Consider replacing complex switch blocks with match where safe
syntax_changes.nullsafe_operator: Use ?-> for nullable chained access in PHP 8+
syntax_changes.constructor_property_promotion: Promote constructor properties in PHP 8+ when appropriate

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description: A TypeError is now thrown when passing object s, resource s, or array s as the boundary inputs.

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description:
A more descriptive ValueError is thrown when
    passing 0 for $step .

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description:
A ValueError is now thrown when using a
    negative $step for increasing ranges.

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description:
If $step is a float that can be interpreted
    as an int, it is now done so.

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description:
A ValueError is now thrown if any argument
    is infinity or NAN.

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description:
An E_WARNING is now emitted if $start or $end is the empty
    string. The value continues to be cast to the value 0 .

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description:
An E_WARNING is now emitted if $start or $end has more than
    one byte, only if it is a non-numeric string.

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description:
An E_WARNING is now emitted if $start or $end is cast to an
    integer because the other boundary input is a number.
    (e.g. range(5, 'z'); ).

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description:
An E_WARNING is now emitted if $step is a float when trying to generate a range of
    characters, except if both boundary inputs are numeric strings (e.g. range('5', '9', 0.5); does not produce a warning).

raw_items.title: PHP: Backward Incompatible Changes - Manual
raw_items.description:
range() now produce a list of characters if one
    of the boundary inputs is a string digit instead of casting the other input
    to int (e.g. range('9', 'A'); ).
